Clinic:
Patient presents for Initial Orthodontic Consultation. Patient age: 16 Patient gender: Female Patient arrived on time? no Minor patient accompanied by: [GUARDIAN] Covering faculty: cole Chief Concern: crooked teeth (specifically UL2) Reviewed MedHx/Significant findings: non contrib Reviewed DentHx/significant findings: up to date as of dec Extra-oral findings: wnl Intraoral findings: cl I R, cl II L mild, mild crowding upper and lower R Molar/Canine Classification: II L Molar/Canine Classification: II TMJ: No Crepitus: No Clicking: No Pain on Palpation: No Hx Trauma: No Sleep questionnaire score and recommendations: none Recommendations: Records to be taken: Yes CBCT with report: Yes IO and EO Photos: Yes Scan/Models Mx Md: Yes INVS Scan: Yes Appt notes/Discussed with patient: patient presents with concern for straightening teeth, requests gold brackets but we don't have that available. DWP tx including brackets, invisalign, and no tx and RBAs of each. recommend orthodontic treatment to improve function and esthetics NV: Consultation of Tx options, Begin Tx upon approval

Clinic:
Patient Presented Today Alone and On Time. Assessed Bite with [DOCTOR], Pt is Class I on both sides with Upper incisors having no clearance with lower brackets. OB 2mm. Complete with Forsus! Removed both forsus on both sides. [DOCTOR] said both were placed reversed (Right in Left side and Left in Right Side). Patient had to go put money in parking meter mid appointment so time was cut short. This appt we removed forsus and religated O Ties. NV: Go to 018 NiTi for the lower to reingage the LL Canine which does not currently engage. Go to 20x20 Upper Archwire with 90 degrees torque for correcting the upper second molar torque. [DOCTOR] says he would like to place those wires himself. Take Photos Next Appt, Ran out of time today

Will wait to do with [DOCTOR] UAW: Kept 19x25 SS Wire LAW: 018 NiTi Elastics: None Appointment notes: Patient presented with UR 6 Band that was broken down the middle of the lingual. Removed Band and Removed the Lace Ties that Patient had from 6 to 6 on both upper and lower. Band was Size 37 LL4 Debonded During Wire Removal Rebonded LL4 with Empower Self Ligating MBT Bracket. Used 018 NiTi to engage in rotated Canines Did not reapply the lace tie (it was needed for forsus to reduce dental side effects) Bonded UR6 Empower Tube Bracket ([DOCTOR] said no need for band since not doing Forsus anymore) Lace Tied the Upper 6-6 Put back same Upper 19x25 Upper SS Wire. Patient Chose Hot Pink O Ties. NV: Torque for Upper 7s with 20x20 SS per what [DOCTOR] Recommended Last Visit.

She says she wears them sometimes at night. Reinforced the need to wear them FULL TIME so we can correct OJ, pt said she will be more compliant. Provided pt with more elastics. Appointment notes: Finishing bends on upper: - Step down bend UR2 - MRT on UR1 - DRT on UL1 Finishing bends on lower: - step down bend on lower centrals - step down bend for LR4 and LL4 Notes: we may consider doing light enamelplasty on the upper centrals to make the incisal edges more rounded. Currently her teeth are very square/masculine. Doing the enamelplasty will aid with smoothening/improving the smile arc NV: PHOTOS, appliance adjustment, assess OJ

Clinic:
Patient presents for appliance adjustment. Patient arrived on time: yes Minor patient accompanied by: * Significant Med/Dental Hx/Changes Med/Dental Hx: no changes Presenting OH: good Alerts if any: * Describe if other alert: * Prophy needed? none Radiogrraph needed? none Faculty assigned: [DOCTOR] Covering faculty if different from assigned; * Compliance: good Molar/Canine: I/I OJ/OB: still 4mm OJ Treatment progress evaluation: slowly UAW: 17x25 TMA LAW: 17x25 TMA Elastics: CS4 Class II spring on the L side. Attached the spring with 014 steel ties between the UL3/UL4 and cinched to the LL6 wing. Appointment notes: Patient has been noncompliant with elastics and so we decided that we will attach the CS4 Class II Dynaflex closed coil spring. Clinic: Seps/ Tx discussion 10y F presents with grandmother, uncle, and younger sister ([NAME]). Went over tx options: 1) MARPE/Facemask - protract maxilla to correct class III using skeletal anchorage, rather than dental anchorage. This has the advantage of maintaining arch length in the upper arch, since she is already crowded. Full fixed tx, evaluate for premolar EXT as needed. 2) RPE/Facemask - protract maxilla with dental anchorage. The downside is that arch length will be decreased as maxilla is protracted, and there is a higher probability of needed EXTs with this tx option 3) Advised that there is still about a chance that we won't be able to correct her bite with ortho alone, in which case she would need orthognathic surgery. This would depend on how she grows, and we will evaluate throughout tx. Pt and grandmother agreed to option 1, MARPE/FM. Placed sep mesial to UR6, could not place mesial to UL6 due to loose ULE. Worked with [DOCTOR]. Pt's uncle and [PATIENT] helped translate for pt's grandmother. NV: Fit bands, impression for MARPE

Clinic: MARPE/FM DELIVERY 10y F presents with grandmother on time for MARPE/facemask delivery. Tried in MARPE and fit well. Cemented bands with bandlock. Pt rinsed with Chlorhexidine 45s. Applied topical anesthetic (TAC gel) on palate. Administered 0.5 carpules of 2% lidocaine with 1:100k epinephrine bilateral greater palatine block. Pt reported anesthesia. Delivered 4 ACR microimplants into palate (1.8mm diameter, 11.0mm length). Pt tolerated procedure well, reported her nose feeling funny. Advised pt to use mouth rinse or salt water rinse to help keep MARPE clean. Pt rinsed with chlorhexidine again for 45 seconds after procedure. Prescribed 2 turns morning, 2 turns at night (0.5mm total/day). Will see pt in 7 days to evaluate expansion. Advised pt to take tylenol if she has pain. Delivered facemask. Pt was able to demonstrate proper facemask wear. Advised 12 hrs/day FM wear. Gave #74 elastics. Worked with [DOCTOR]. NV: 1 week evaluation
